definition: Credit cycle position is the primary severity discriminant for carry unwind events. Tight credit produces contained unwinds, turning credit produces amplified unwinds, and late-cycle credit produces systemic unwinds. Functions as a near-perfect classifier across 6+ episodes from 1992-2024.
evidence: Current IG ~110bp and HY ~380bp place next carry unwind toward contained classification
pair_0 debate judged this the most empirically robust claim with convergence from completely different analytical lenses
generalist_01 calls it a perfect classifier; generalist_02 derives same conclusion from three-tier historical severity hierarchy
open_questions: Does the 2025-2027 maturity wall introduce sufficient time-varying risk to shift classification?
At what credit spread thresholds do regime transitions occur?
Is the classifier robust to simultaneous BoJ normalization and credit deterioration?
